2021-04-01.title: Investigators say a Trump aide took it upon himself to award hefty pandemic contracts.
2021-04-01.abstract: Peter Navarro, frustrated by the snail’s pace response to Covid-19, gave contracts worth hundreds of millions of dollars to politically connected or novice companies, according to a House inquiry.

2021-04-01.title: For many college students, pandemic life is disappointing. For others, it is a financial crisis.
2021-04-01.abstract: As campuses struggle under the shadow of the pandemic, many students report having trouble paying for food, housing and other basic needs.

2021-04-01.title: Hong Kong Court Convicts Democracy Leaders Over Protest March
2021-04-01.abstract: The defendants, including the media tycoon Jimmy Lai and the barristers Martin Lee and Margaret Ng, were some of the city’s most prominent activists.

2021-04-01.title: Women Are Calling Out ‘Rape Culture’ in U.K. Schools
2021-04-01.abstract: Thousands of anonymous online accounts have shined a spotlight on sexual violence against young women and girls.

2021-04-02.title: Taiwan Train Crash Kills Dozens in Deadliest Accident in Decades
2021-04-02.abstract: The Taroko Express train derailed and crashed in a tunnel on the first day of a long holiday weekend.

2021-04-02.title: Your Friday Briefing
2021-04-02.abstract: The challenging latest chapter of the pandemic.

2021-04-02.title: Shut Out on Vaccines, Tiny San Marino Turns to Old Friend: Russia
2021-04-02.abstract: A microstate surrounded by Italy, San Marino feared being left behind in Europe’s inoculation campaign. Now it has jumped ahead, with the Sputnik vaccine sent by an unlikely, faraway friend.

2021-04-02.title: Is the Fight Against Sexism in Australia’s Politics Different This Time?
2021-04-02.abstract: Six weeks after Brittany Higgins spoke up with her allegation of rape in the defense minister’s office, some things have changed and some things have not.

2021-04-02.title: Vaccine ‘Fiasco’ Damages Europe’s Credibility
2021-04-02.abstract: The European Union’s failure to secure adequate vaccine supplies, followed by an export ban, has dented the reputation of the bloc’s leaders. It may also hurt their ability to act in other areas.

2021-04-03.title: ‘Like a Miracle’: Israel’s Vaccine Success Allows Easter Crowds in Jerusalem
2021-04-03.abstract: Last year, the pandemic forced the cancellation of the Good Friday procession in Jerusalem. This Holy Week, local Christians are thronging the streets of the Old City.

2021-04-03.title: With Swarms of Ships, Beijing Tightens Its Grip on South China Sea
2021-04-03.abstract: After building artificial islands, China is using large fleets of ostensibly civilian boats to press other countries’ vessels out of disputed waters.

2021-04-03.title: In Turkey’s Failed Coup, Trainees Face the Same Stiff Punishments as Generals
2021-04-03.abstract: The families of trainee pilots sentenced to life in prison broke their silence to protest the men’s innocence. The pilots are among more than 600 trainees and conscripts swept up in prosecutions.

2021-04-03.title: Women, 86 Percent Absent From Jordan’s Work Force, Are Left Behind
2021-04-03.abstract: “As long as women are absent from the labor market, they are absent from the public sphere,” one advocate said.

2021-04-03.title: More than 3 million people are now receiving a Covid vaccine in the U.S. each day.
2021-04-03.abstract: The milestone, which comes amid a worrying resurgence of variants in certain parts of the country, reflects a steady increase in the capacity of states to deliver shots into arms.

2021-04-04.title: ‘She Just Fell Down. And She Died.’
2021-04-04.abstract: Myanmar’s security forces have killed more than 40 children since February. Here is the story of one, Aye Myat Thu. She was 10.

2021-04-04.title: An Outspoken Student Union Positions Itself at the Vanguard of a Changing France
2021-04-04.abstract: To its critics, the 114-year-old Unef is the incarnation of the American-inspired ideas that threaten France’s founding principles. Its leaders say it is the future.

2021-04-04.title: Taiwan Crash Investigators Focus on How Truck Fell in Train’s Path
2021-04-04.abstract: Officials were trying to determine why the truck slid downhill from a construction site on Friday, resulting in the island’s deadliest rail disaster in decades.

2021-04-04.title: Floods and Mudslides in Eastern Indonesia Leave at Least 41 Dead
2021-04-04.abstract: Entire neighborhoods were submerged, with some of the worst effects hitting a remote island accessible only by sea, officials said.

2021-04-04.title: U.S. Looks to Build on Secret Portions of Taliban Deal to Reduce Violence
2021-04-04.abstract: In the final weeks before the U.S. is set to withdraw from Afghanistan, officials are trying to negotiate with the insurgent group and stave off a Taliban spring offensive.

2021-04-05.title: My Life in Israel’s Brave New Post-Pandemic Future
2021-04-05.abstract: A Green Pass allows us, the vaccinated, to go to concerts, restaurants and sporting events. But Israel’s real-time experiment in post-lockdown living leaves many questions unanswered.

2021-04-05.title: Your Monday Briefing
2021-04-05.abstract: Debate in France over its student union.

2021-04-05.title: Israel offers a hint of what post-pandemic life may look like.
2021-04-05.abstract: Israel’s vaccination program has been remarkably swift and successful. In recent weeks, new coronavirus infections have dropped significantly, from a peak of 10,000 a day in January to a few hundred by late March. The economy has almost fully reopened.

2021-04-05.title: Millions of white evangelicals do not intend to get vaccinated.
2021-04-05.abstract: Across white evangelical America, reasons not to get vaccinated have spread quickly.

2021-04-05.title: The plan for post-pandemic life in England includes free testing and Covid certificates.
2021-04-05.abstract: Prime Minister Boris Johnson of Britain offered a first detailed glimpse of what a post-pandemic society in England might look like on Monday, announcing free twice-weekly coronavirus tests and Covid status certificates that would allow people with immunity into crowded nightclubs and sporting events.

2021-04-06.title: North Korea, citing the pandemic, will skip the Tokyo Olympics.
2021-04-06.abstract: North Korea, which has a decrepit public health system, has taken stringent measures against the virus since early last year, including shutting its borders.

2021-04-06.title: Your Tuesday Briefing
2021-04-06.abstract: Calls for a global minimum corporate tax.

2021-04-06.title: He Led Hitler’s Secret Police in Austria. Then He Spied for the West.
2021-04-06.abstract: Franz Josef Huber, responsible for deporting tens of thousands of Jews, escaped punishment with U.S. backing and went on to work for West German intelligence, newly disclosed records reveal.

2021-04-06.title: Netanyahu Gets First Crack at Forming a New Government in Israel
2021-04-06.abstract: After a fourth inconclusive election in two years, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu now has a possible path to stay in office even as he stands trial on corruption charges.

2021-04-06.title: How Will China Vaccinate 560 Million People? Start With Free Ice Cream.
2021-04-06.abstract: China’s Covid-19 vaccination campaign got off to a slow start. It is now trying to catch up, through a mixture of freebies and the occasional threat.

2021-04-07.title: In Bid to Boost Its Profile, ISIS Turns to Africa’s Militants
2021-04-07.abstract: Violence by Islamist extremists in Africa reached a record high last year. Now, the Islamic State is using those attacks to project an image of strength.

2021-04-07.title: Couple Who Defaced $400,000 Painting Thought It Was a Public Art Project
2021-04-07.abstract: The vandalism of a piece by the graffiti artist JonOne at a gallery in South Korea has prompted a debate about contemporary art.

2021-04-07.title: Two Presidents Visited Turkey. Only the Man Was Offered a Chair.
2021-04-07.abstract: A protocol mishap involving Ursula von der Leyen, the European Commission president, was cited by critics as symbolic of Turkey’s treatment of women. It also underlined divisions within the European Union.

2021-04-07.title: U.K. says under-30s should get a non-AstraZeneca option; E.U. finds a ‘possible link’ to rare clots.
2021-04-07.abstract: Britain said on Wednesday that it would offer alternatives to the AstraZeneca vaccine for adults under 30 as European regulators described a “possible link” with rare blood clots, a setback for the world’s most widely used vaccine and a blow to the more than 100 countries relying on it to save lives amid a global surge in coronavirus cases.

2021-04-08.title: Myanmar’s Ambassador Locked Out of London Embassy
2021-04-08.abstract: Kyaw Zwar Minn, Myanmar’s ambassador to Britain, said he was locked out of the embassy in London on Wednesday, apparently for criticizing the Burmese military, which staged a coup in February.

2021-04-08.title: Powerful Men Fall, One After Another, in France’s Delayed #MeToo
2021-04-08.abstract: Since the start of the year, well-known men from diverse fields have been accused of sexual abuse and placed under investigation.

2021-04-08.title: Amid Sexual Harassment Scandals, Australia Plots a ‘Road Map for Respect’
2021-04-08.abstract: Prime Minister Scott Morrison said he would accept 55 suggestions from the country’s human rights commissioner to tackle a problem that has been festering for years in politics.

2021-04-08.title: ‘No More Parties’: Mexico’s Piñata Makers Badly Bruised by Pandemic
2021-04-08.abstract: The piñata industry, dependent on social gatherings, has seen sales plummet. Some artisans, in a creative bid to survive, have added coronavirus figures to their lineups of superheros and princesses.

2021-04-09.title: Young Women Are Dropping Out of School and Work. Is Caregiving the Culprit?
2021-04-09.abstract: While much of the economy is beginning to bounce back, young people — particularly young women — are living a different reality.

2021-04-09.title: Rise of Variants in Europe Shows How Dangerous the Virus Can Be
2021-04-09.abstract: Much of the continent is experiencing a wave of outbreaks driven by the B.1.1.7 variant, which ravaged Britain this winter.

2021-04-09.title: Your Friday Briefing
2021-04-09.abstract: The dangerous rise of coronavirus variants.

2021-04-09.title: Chinese Reality Shows Censor Western Clothing Brands
2021-04-09.abstract: Online platforms that stream dance, singing and comedy shows are pixelating performers’ T-shirts and sneakers amid a nationalistic fervor.

2021-04-09.title: Where Boars Hog the Streets
2021-04-09.abstract: Groups of boars have become an unavoidable presence in Haifa. Some human residents are charmed, but others are annoyed or frightened and now carry sticks on walks.

2021-04-10.title: World Leaders Remember Prince Philip
2021-04-10.abstract: Leaders from around the world paid tribute to the life and service of Prince Philip, the Duke of Edinburgh, who died on Friday, and offered condolences to Queen Elizabeth II and the royal family.

2021-04-10.title: Royal Rivalry Bares Social Tensions Behind Jordan’s Stable Veneer
2021-04-10.abstract: The standoff between King Abdullah II and his half brother, Prince Hamzah, wasn’t just palace intrigue. It hinted at Saudi meddling and highlighted wider tensions within Jordan’s complex society.

2021-04-10.title: Harry Will Attend Philip’s Funeral, Raising Hope Royal Rift Will Heal
2021-04-10.abstract: Although Harry  will come home to mourn with the family, his pregnant wife Meghan will not. The patriarch’s death opens an uncertain chapter for the House of Windsor.

2021-04-10.title: ‘You Can’t Trust Anyone’: Russia’s Hidden Covid Toll Is an Open Secret
2021-04-10.abstract: The country’s official coronavirus death toll is 102,649. But at least 300,000 more people died last year during the pandemic than were reported in Russia’s most widely cited official statistics.

2021-04-10.title: A Soviet ‘Lord of the Rings’ Is Unearthed, Epic in Its Own Way
2021-04-10.abstract: Tolkien fans received an unexpected gift with the rediscovery of an all-but-forgotten 1991 production. They were also left with questions, like “why is Gollum wearing a lettuce on his head?”

2021-04-11.title: ‘Tell Us if He’s Dead’: Abductions and Torture Rattle Uganda
2021-04-11.abstract: Hundreds have been detained, many brutalized, after a bloody, contested election. The government of Yoweri Museveni appears intent on breaking the back of the opposition.

2021-04-11.title: ‘Everything Is Worth Freedom’: Uganda’s Opposition Leader Faces the Future
2021-04-11.abstract: Bobi Wine, the pop star who became a presidential candidate, considers rebuilding his anti-government movement after a violent election season that left many aides and supporters imprisoned.

2021-04-11.title: Conservative Ex-Banker Headed to Victory in Presidential Election in Ecuador
2021-04-11.abstract: Guillermo Lasso was ahead of Andrés Arauz late Sunday night in an election that saw the emergence of the country’s long marginalized Indigenous movement as a key driver of the political conversation.

2021-04-11.title: In Peru’s Presidential Election, the Most Popular Choice Is No One
2021-04-11.abstract: Peruvians head to the polls at a moment that many are calling one of the lowest points in the country’s young democracy, and many plan to cast empty ballots.

2021-04-11.title: Chinese official acknowledges low effectiveness of the country’s Covid vaccines.
2021-04-11.abstract: A senior Chinese official said that the country’s vaccines may need to be administered in greater doses or in concert with other shots because of their low overall effectiveness.

2021-04-12.title: Myanmar Coup Puts the Seal on Autocracy’s Rise in Southeast Asia
2021-04-12.abstract: Not long ago, democracy seemed to be surging in the region. But in Thailand, Malaysia, the Philippines and elsewhere, it is in trouble.

2021-04-12.title: England reopens shops and some pubs and restaurants after three months of lockdown.
2021-04-12.abstract: Britain reopened large parts of its economy on Monday, allowing people in England back in shops, hair salons and outdoor areas of pubs and restaurants, a long-awaited milestone after three months of lockdown, and a day after the country recorded its lowest daily coronavirus death toll since September.

2021-04-12.title: When Anti-Asian Parody Targeted BTS, the Boy Band’s Fan Army Mobilized
2021-04-12.abstract: A global outcry fueled by a sketch on a Chilean comedy show reflected a growing sensitivity to racist, particularly anti-Asian, speech.

2021-04-12.title: After Nuclear Site Blackout, Thunder From Iran, and Silence From U.S.
2021-04-12.abstract: The Americans and Israelis have worked together in the past to impede Iran’s nuclear ambitions, but the U.S. denies that was the case in the latest sabotage.

2021-04-13.title: Taiwan Hunters Contend With Taboos, and Trials, to Uphold Tradition
2021-04-13.abstract: The island’s Indigenous hunting cultures are circumscribed by ancient rituals and modern legal restrictions. We join a hunt as Taiwan’s constitutional court considers a case on Indigenous rights.

2021-04-13.title: The virus is surging in the Northeast and Upper Midwest, while other regions are largely keeping it at bay.
2021-04-13.abstract: Experts aren’t sure what explains the divide; some think warmer weather in the Sun Belt has played a role, while others suspect decreased testing is muddying the virus’s true footprint.

2021-04-13.title: The Only Ones Arrested After a Child’s Rape: The Women Who Helped Her
2021-04-13.abstract: The assault of a 13-year-old girl in Venezuela and the arrest of her mother and a teacher who helped her end the pregnancy have forced a national debate about legalizing abortion.

2021-04-13.title: In the U.K., vaccines are being rolled out to younger groups as the program continues at pace.
2021-04-13.abstract: Britain has now offered vaccinations to everyone in the country age 50 and older, the government announced late on Monday, and is extending its program to another age group, the latest sign that the national rollout is continuing at pace.

2021-04-14.title: NATO Chief Criticizes Russian Military Buildup
2021-04-14.abstract: Jens Stoltenberg, the NATO secretary general, expressed concern over Russia’s increasing military presence along the border with Ukraine, calling it “unexplained and unjustified.”

2021-04-14.title: Your Wednesday Briefing
2021-04-14.abstract: A fresh blow to Europe’s vaccination efforts.

2021-04-14.title: They ‘Bombed My Dream’: Denmark Strips Some Syrians of Residency Status
2021-04-14.abstract: The country is the first E.U. nation to make such a move. Many Syrians say that returning to their native country isn’t an option, and rights groups warn that the policy will tear some families apart.

2021-04-14.title: With 100 days till the Olympics, Japan grapples with a new wave of infections.
2021-04-14.abstract: A subdued ceremony celebrated the approach of the Games in Tokyo, parts of which entered a quasi-state of emergency last week.

2021-04-14.title: Highlighting a rare potential vaccine side effect has risks of its own, public health officials warn.
2021-04-14.abstract: To federal health officials, asking states on Tuesday to suspend use of the Johnson & Johnson coronavirus vaccine until they could investigate six extremely rare but troubling cases of blood clots was an obvious and perhaps unavoidable move.

2021-04-15.title: Will Japan Confront China? A Visit to Washington May Offer a Clue.
2021-04-15.abstract: Prime Minister Yoshihide Suga is the first foreign leader to be invited to the Biden White House, where he is likely to be pressed to more fully address Beijing’s threat to stability in the region.

2021-04-15.title: How Mario Draghi Is Making Italy a Power Player in Europe
2021-04-15.abstract: The new prime minister is leveraging his European relationships and his solid reputation to make Italy a force on the continent in a way it has not been in decades.

2021-04-15.title: A top politician in Japan talked of canceling the Olympics. Then he walked it back.
2021-04-15.abstract: A leading member of Japan’s governing party said the country might have to “completely cancel” the Games if coronavirus infections continue to surge.

2021-04-15.title: Key Chains and Teddy Bears in Riot Gear: Hong Kong Promotes National Security
2021-04-15.abstract: The government organized a day of citywide activities, from schools to the police academy, to encourage residents to support a recent national security law.

2021-04-16.title: Disappearing Slang and Cultural Variants: How Australian English Is Changing
2021-04-16.abstract: Readers reflect on the country’s many different accents.

2021-04-16.title: Paraguay’s ‘Life and Death’ Covid Crisis Gives China Diplomatic Opening
2021-04-16.abstract: Paraguay desperately needs Covid-19 vaccines, which China could supply. That has led the South American nation to reconsider its relationship with Taiwan, which precludes it from dealing with Beijing.

2021-04-16.title: Hong Kong Court Sentences Jimmy Lai and Other Pro-Democracy Leaders to Prison
2021-04-16.abstract: The punishments for Mr. Lai, a media tycoon, and other leaders over an unauthorized peaceful protest point to what critics say is the shrinking space for dissent in Hong Kong.

2021-04-16.title: Booster shots and re-vaccinations could be needed. Drug companies are planning for it.
2021-04-16.abstract: Pfizer’s chief executive said a third dose of the company’s Covid-19 vaccine was “likely” to be needed within a year of the initial two-dose inoculation, followed by annual vaccinations.

2021-04-16.title: Overwhelmed by the virus, Paraguay considers ditching Taiwan for China — and its vaccines.
2021-04-16.abstract: In Paraguay, the government of Taiwan has built thousands of homes for the poor, upgraded the health care system, awarded hundreds of scholarships and helped fund a futuristic Congress building. But the alliance is facing an existential threat as Paraguay’s quest for Covid-19 vaccines becomes increasingly desperate.

2021-04-17.title: Scrutiny of William and Harry’s relationship hovers over the funeral.
2021-04-17.abstract: Almost 24 years ago, the world watched as a pair of brothers, ages 15 and 12, walked a mile through London behind a horse-drawn carriage holding their mother’s coffin.

2021-04-17.title: Philip’s funeral nods to a life of service and his ‘unwavering loyalty’ to the queen.
2021-04-17.abstract: Queen Elizabeth II, wearing a black mask and seated alone, said goodbye to her husband of more than 73 years, Prince Philip, the Duke of Edinburgh, at his funeral on Saturday at St. George’s Chapel in Windsor Castle.

2021-04-17.title: These Countries Did Well With Covid. So Why Are They Slow on Vaccines?
2021-04-17.abstract: Japan, South Korea and Australia have inoculated tiny percentages of their populations. The delays risk unwinding their relative successes.

2021-04-17.title: Myanmar’s Protesters Face Down the Military With Slingshots and Rocks
2021-04-17.abstract: What began as peaceful demonstrations against the military coup has rapidly grown into a resistance movement in which citizens use improvised weapons to fight the junta.

2021-04-17.title: With attendance limited to 30, a look at who was at the funeral.
2021-04-17.abstract: Amid coronavirus restrictions, Britain has had to adjust how it grieves over the past year. And with current rules allowing for just 30 people at funerals, the royal family scaled back plans for the service for Prince Philip.

2021-04-18.title: Czechs Blame 2014 Blasts at Ammunition Depots on Elite Russian Spy Unit
2021-04-18.abstract: The unit has also been implicated in the 2018 nerve agent attack in Salisbury, England, on Sergei Skripal, a former Russian spy, and his daughter.

2021-04-18.title: Despite Tensions, U.S. and China Agree to Work Together on Climate Change
2021-04-18.abstract: The two countries said they would treat global warming “with the seriousness and urgency that it demands.”

2021-04-18.title: How the Tiny Kingdom of Bhutan Out-Vaccinated Most of the World
2021-04-18.abstract: The Himalayan nation has given more than 60 percent of its people a shot. Some villages were reached by helicopter, and health workers hiked through ice and snow.

2021-04-18.title: After ‘Green Rush,’ Canada’s Legal Pot Suppliers Are Stumbling
2021-04-18.abstract: Most marijuana producers in Canada are still reporting staggering losses two and a half years after legalization.

2021-04-18.title: Afghan Women Fear the Worst, Whether War or Peace Lies Ahead
2021-04-18.abstract: Whatever happens once the United States withdraws will not bode well for Afghan women. But even the gains made for them over the last 20 years have often been fleeting.

2021-04-19.title: Russia Expels 20 Czech Diplomats as Tensions Escalate
2021-04-19.abstract: On Saturday, Prague kicked out 18 Russian diplomats after accusing Russia of being behind an attack on an ammunition depot. On Sunday, Moscow retaliated.

2021-04-19.title: A Cuba Without a Castro? A Country Steps Into the Unknown.
2021-04-19.abstract: Raúl Castro has stepped down as head of Cuba’s Communist Party, leaving Cubans without a Castro to lead them for the first time in over 60 years.

2021-04-19.title: A Quiet Arms Race Is Rapidly Heating Up Between the Two Koreas
2021-04-19.abstract: The buildup over the last few years has threatened the delicate balance of peace on the Korean Peninsula.

2021-04-19.title: Fleeing a Modern War, Syrians Seek Refuge in Ancient Ruins
2021-04-19.abstract: So many people have fled to Syria’s crowded northwest that families have settled in important archaeological sites. “We, too, have become ruins.”

2021-04-20.title: Wildfire Rages on Table Mountain in Cape Town
2021-04-20.abstract: Strong winds fanned flames across the slopes of Table Mountain and onto the University of Cape Town campus, destroying a library housing priceless pieces of South African history.

2021-04-20.title: Your Tuesday Briefing
2021-04-20.abstract: Wider vaccine eligibility in the U.S.

2021-04-20.title: ‘A Threat From the Russian State’: Ukrainians Alarmed as Troops Mass on Their Doorstep
2021-04-20.abstract: Few analysts believe that Moscow intends to invade. But as Russia’s military buildup proceeds, the tension is rising in war-weary Eastern Ukraine.

2021-04-20.title: Iran Rattled as Israel Repeatedly Strikes Key Targets
2021-04-20.abstract: Recent attacks suggest that Israel has a clandestine network inside Iran and that Iranian security services have been powerless to break it.

2021-04-20.title: What Makes a Happy Country?
2021-04-20.abstract: Finland, for the fourth consecutive year, topped a list of countries evaluated on the well-being of their inhabitants. “Really?” Finns ask.

2021-04-21.title: Your Wednesday Briefing
2021-04-21.abstract: A jury finds Derek Chauvin guilty.

2021-04-21.title: After Trump ‘Failed,’ South Korean Leader Hopes Biden Can Salvage Nuclear Deal
2021-04-21.abstract: In an interview with The New York Times, President Moon Jae-in of South Korea urged the United States to sit down with North Korea.

2021-04-21.title: How Phone Scammers Talked Her Into Handing Over $33 Million
2021-04-21.abstract: In one of the most staggering Hong Kong phone cons, a 90-year-old woman was swindled over five months.

2021-04-21.title: Putin says nations that threaten Russia’s security will ‘regret their deeds.’
2021-04-21.abstract: President Vladimir V. Putin of Russia on Wednesday delivered an annual address replete with threats against the West but, despite intense tensions with Ukraine, stopped short of announcing new military or foreign policy moves.

2021-04-21.title: Navalny says he’s ‘a skeleton walking’ as worries grow about his medical condition in prison.
2021-04-21.abstract: Protests are starting to unfold across Russia as activists demand the release of the opposition leader from prison.

2021-04-22.title: India sets a global record for daily infections.
2021-04-22.abstract: More than 310,000 cases were recorded in just 24 hours, surpassing the previous single-day high set in the United States in January.

2021-04-22.title: Where Did Chad Rebels Prepare for Their Own War? In Libya.
2021-04-22.abstract: Fighting as mercenaries for years, the rebels were ready to pull off their own stunning feat: an invasion that resulted in the death of Chad’s strongman ruler.

2021-04-22.title: Guilty Verdict for Hong Kong Journalist as Media Faces ‘Frontal Assault’
2021-04-22.abstract: As China stifles dissent in the city, news outlets have found themselves in the authorities’ cross hairs.

2021-04-22.title: For Extra Days Off, Officials Say, Couple Had 4 Weddings and 3 Divorces
2021-04-22.abstract: A bank in Taiwan said an employee had a rapid succession of nuptials to the same woman to take advantage of the country’s paid marriage leave.

2021-04-23.title: Your Friday Briefing
2021-04-23.abstract: New commitments to combat climate change.

2021-04-23.title: From Mao Zedong to Xi Jinping
2021-04-23.abstract: A former Beijing bureau chief shares insights from her time in China.

2021-04-23.title: China’s Top Mixed Martial Arts Star Isn’t Fighting for a Cause
2021-04-23.abstract: In China, she’s a symbol of women’s rights and a national hero. But Zhang Weili, who defends her U.F.C. title in Florida on Saturday, cares about just one label: champion.

2021-04-23.title: 1971: German Diplomats Frustrated by Edward Kennedy’s Tardiness
2021-04-23.abstract: The American senator and his wife showed up late to various appointments during their visit. It made a bad impression on their hosts.

2021-04-23.title: Ravaged by Covid, Brazil Faces a Hunger Epidemic
2021-04-23.abstract: Tens of millions of Brazilians are facing hunger or food insecurity as the country’s Covid-19 crisis drags on, killing thousands of people every day.

2021-04-24.title: India Faces Record-Breaking Virus Surge
2021-04-24.abstract: India set a global record for new daily coronavirus infections on Friday, beating the previous record from the day before, while oxygen supplies dwindled.

2021-04-24.title: General Who Led Myanmar’s Coup Joins Regional Talks on the Crisis
2021-04-24.abstract: Southeast Asian leaders called for the killings to end. But critics feared Senior Gen. Min Aung Hlaing’s presence at the Indonesia talks would legitimize his rule.

2021-04-24.title: In Poland, an L.G.B.T.Q. Migration As Homophobia Deepens
2021-04-24.abstract: An escalation in verbal attacks by the Polish government, with the support of the Roman Catholic Church, as well as the threat of physical violence on the streets of many cities, has triggered an exodus of gay people.

2021-04-24.title: Debris From Indonesian Submarine Is Found, Ending Hopes of Rescue
2021-04-24.abstract: Search teams from several countries had been looking for the navy vessel, which sank near Bali with 53 people aboard.

2021-04-24.title: As Covid-19 Devastates India, Deaths Go Undercounted
2021-04-24.abstract: Fatalities have been overlooked or downplayed, understating the human toll of the country’s outbreak, which accounts for nearly half of all new cases in a global surge.

2021-04-25.title: New Zealand hosts 50,000 fans in its largest concert since the pandemic began.
2021-04-25.abstract: AUCKLAND, New Zealand — More than 50,000 fans packed into Eden Park stadium, New Zealand’s largest, on Saturday night for what is believed to be the largest live in-person concert since the pandemic began.

2021-04-25.title: Spanish police arrested a man for spreading the coronavirus to 22 people.
2021-04-25.abstract: The National Police in Spain said on Saturday that they had arrested a man who went to his workplace and a gym while showing Covid-19 symptoms, spreading the virus to 22 people.

2021-04-25.title: Phuket Was Poised for Tourism Comeback. A Covid Surge Dashed Those Hopes.
2021-04-25.abstract: Phuket, one of Thailand’s top tourist havens, had an ambitious plan to reopen to the world this summer. But with a spike in cases, the island’s desperate situation is unlikely to end anytime soon.

2021-04-25.title: Fire at Baghdad Hospital Packed With Covid Patients Kills at Least 82
2021-04-25.abstract: A blaze set off by an exploding oxygen cylinder swept through a hospital that lacked smoke detectors or sprinklers. More are expected to die from severe burns.

2021-04-25.title: Cases remain high in the U.S., but vaccinations are slowing.
2021-04-25.abstract: The average number of vaccine shots given each day, which rose for months and peaked at 3.38 million, has now fallen to 2.86 million, the lowest level since March 31.

2021-04-26.title: Iran’s Foreign Minister, in Leaked Tape, Says Revolutionary Guards Set Policies
2021-04-26.abstract: Mohammad Javad Zarif, the Iranian foreign minister, described a rivalry with a powerful and widely revered military leader, Qassim Suleimani.

2021-04-26.title: Terrorist Group Steps Into Venezuela as Lawlessness Grows
2021-04-26.abstract: With Venezuela in shambles, criminals and insurgents run large stretches of the nation’s territory. We traveled through one of the regions under their control.

2021-04-26.title: Italy’s Problem With School Dropouts Goes From Bad to Worse in Pandemic
2021-04-26.abstract: Even before the coronavirus hit, Italy had among the worst dropout rates in Europe. But closed schools and online classes have pushed students out in ever higher numbers.

2021-04-26.title: India continues to shatter case records as it awaits international aid.
2021-04-26.abstract: India’s coronavirus crisis deepened on Monday with the number of new reported cases setting a global record for the fifth consecutive day, as countries, companies and members of the large diaspora pledged to send oxygen and other critical aid.

2021-04-27.title: West Virginia will give $100 savings bonds to people 16 to 35 who get vaccinated.
2021-04-27.abstract: The governor said he hoped the payments, which will come from federal stimulus money, would motivate younger residents to get inoculated.

2021-04-27.title: Brazil’s health authority rejects importing Russia’s Sputnik V vaccine.
2021-04-27.abstract: RIO DE JANEIRO — Brazil’s health authority said late Monday that it would not recommend importing Sputnik V, the Covid-19 vaccine developed by Russia.

2021-04-27.title: Your Tuesday Briefing
2021-04-27.abstract: Travel to the E.U. for vaccinated Americans.

2021-04-27.title: Greek Neo-Nazi Lawmaker Stripped of Immunity by European Parliament
2021-04-27.abstract: Ioannis Lagos, sentenced to 13 years in prison by a Greek court for his part in running the fascist Golden Dawn party, had been immune from extradition as an elected member of the European Union’s legislature.

2021-04-27.title: A Graying China May Have to Put Off Retirement. Workers Aren’t Happy.
2021-04-27.abstract: Most Chinese workers retire by 60. But with the population aging and pension funds running low, the government says that must change.

2021-04-28.title: Hungary Transfers 11 Universities to Foundations Led by Orban Allies
2021-04-28.abstract: The transfer, accompanied by billions of euros in state assets, will enable the prime minister and his supporters to exert long-term influence.

2021-04-28.title: How Europe Sealed a Pfizer Vaccine Deal With Texts and Calls
2021-04-28.abstract: The European Union is about to sign a deal for 1.8 billion doses of the Pfizer-BioNTech vaccine after a dispute with AstraZeneca derailed its vaccination campaign. Here’s how it came about.

2021-04-28.title: Australian Who Filmed 4 Dead and Dying Officers Gets Prison Sentence
2021-04-28.abstract: The judge described the filming of police officers who had been hit by a truck on a freeway as “heartless, cruel and disgraceful.”

2021-04-28.title: Ten Years Ago, Ed Balls Tweeted ‘Ed Balls.’ How Is It Still Funny?
2021-04-28.abstract: A decade is an eternity in internet years. But people still haven’t forgotten the British politician’s errant tweet, and he’s still playing along.

2021-04-29.title: Crossing the Niger River, Inch by Painful Inch, Hour by Wasted Hour
2021-04-29.abstract: A narrow, old bridge that connects two economically vital areas of Nigeria is a chokepoint stifling progress in Africa’s most populous nation.

2021-04-29.title: ‘They Call It Social Cleansing’: Court May Force Honduras to Better Protect Trans People
2021-04-29.abstract: Outraged by a long-ignored slaying in Honduras, lawyers are urging a human rights court in Central America to force governments to better protect transgender people in a region where they are targets.

2021-04-29.title: New York Times photographers capture families reuniting at nursing homes.
2021-04-29.abstract: A daughter holding her mother’s hand. A son overcome that his 95-year-old mother had survived the pandemic. A stoic family patriarch, suddenly in tears.

2021-04-29.title: Navalny’s Network Crumbling Under Kremlin Pressure
2021-04-29.abstract: As the jailed opposition leader made his first public appearance since a hunger strike, his associates said government pressure caused them to shut down regional offices.

2021-04-30.title: Stampede at Israel Religious Celebration Kills at Least 45
2021-04-30.abstract: An estimated 100,000 people had gathered on Mount Meron to celebrate a religious holiday. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu called it a “terrible disaster.”

2021-04-30.title: What to Know About India’s Coronavirus Crisis
2021-04-30.abstract: What is behind the explosion of new coronavirus cases that is overwhelming the South Asian country?

2021-04-30.title: What is Australian Food? A New Cookbook Provides Some Answers.
2021-04-30.abstract: An ambitious new book from Phaidon chronicles three periods of Australian cuisine, from the ancient past to the Anglo era after colonization to the multicultural present.

2021-04-30.title: ‘Now We Are United’: Myanmar’s Ethnic Divisions Soften After Coup
2021-04-30.abstract: Amid the resistance to military rule, some are saying that democracy can’t flourish without respecting the minorities that have been persecuted for decades.
